title: George Celtics catch Vultures by surprise
description: GEORGE NEWS - The Rosemoor-based side George...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The Rosemoor-based side George Celtics secured a 1-1 draw against Thembalethu outfit George Vultures in a Promotional/Development League encounter on Sunday morning, 23 June at the Lawaaikamp Stadium.


> With 33 points, George Celtics are currently in third position on the Promotional League log table.


They have played 15 games with ten wins, three draws and two defeats. George Vultures are battling to reach the top four. Shakeal Malapan of George Celtics was played an outstanding game - steering on attack and using great touches on the ball, and his award came when he managed to score for his side.

Though there were no goals in the first half, it was a tough face-off with both teams playing a well-balanced game attacking on the flanks. George Celtics were using their physical strength on the ball while the Vultures back line did very well on defence.

In the second half, Vultures played at a disadvantage after their goalkeeper, Khanyani Nobhakada, was sent off with a red card for a dangerous challenge outside the box. The first goal was scored late in the second half by Samkele Klaas who capitalised on a free kick, with the equaliser coming from Shakeal Malapan scoring from a penalty in the last minutes of the game.

Promotional League results

Saturday 22 June

Future Stars 2-1 Southern Academy

Paris United 2-1 Tigers

Vista 2-3 France United

Vuvuzela 1-3 Spurs United

Sunday 23 June

George Celtics 1-1 George Vultures

Young Masters 3-1 Burning Fire

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
